---
title: "Week 8: Text as Data IV"
published-title: week_8
engine: knitr
freeze: false
---

This lecture answers the following questions:

- What's the latest and greatest in Natural Language Processing (NLP)?
- What do these new methods bring to the table?
- Which requirements do they have?

## Slides 

Please click [here to download the latest version of the slides](./sli20241210-tad-IV.pdf).

```{css echo=FALSE}

.embed-container {
    position: relative;
    padding-bottom: 129%;
    height: 0;
    overflow: hidden;
    max-width: 100%;
}

.embed-container iframe,
.embed-container object,
.embed-container embed {
    position: absolute;
    top: 0;
    left: 0;
    width: 100%;
    height: 100%;
}

```

Alternatively, read it here (probably not the best if you're visiting this page on a mobile device):

<div class="embed-container">
  <iframe src="./slides/20241210-tad-IV.pdf" style="border: 0.5px"></iframe>
</div>
